venue: Madison Square Garden, New York, NY
game_result_summary: New York erased a 22-point fourth-quarter deficit, tied the game late in regulation, then opened overtime with a decisive burst to win 115-104. Cleveland controlled most of the middle game but collapsed offensively and structurally after leading 93-71 with 7:52 left in the fourth.

position_results.ticket: No - New York wins by over 9.5
No - New York wins by over 8.5

betting_result_summary.primary_failure: We correctly identified the game as competitive through regulation, but failed to protect against overtime margin expansion after a massive Knicks comeback.

first_quarter.read: Knicks defense and bench pressure created early separation despite poor spacing. Cleveland committed five turnovers and shot 27.3% from the field.
first_quarter.model_note: Early Cleveland turnover pressure should have raised Knicks comeback and chaos-tail probability, but not enough to justify preemptive margin expansion protection.

second_quarter.read: Cleveland flipped the game with elite shotmaking: 10/16 FG and 6/9 from three in the quarter. Mitchell and Harden powered the run, but Harden turnovers remained a warning flag.
second_quarter.model_note: Halftime read overweighted Cleveland's control and underweighted the cost of 11 first-half Cavs turnovers.

third_quarter.read: Cleveland appeared to seize full control behind Mitchell shot creation, Mobley/Allen interior stability, and Knicks free-throw/shot-quality instability. NYK entered the fourth down 83-69.
third_quarter.model_note: At 83-69, Cavs had the correct live side profile, but the model should have activated fourth-quarter closeout fragility due to Harden usage drag and Mitchell fatigue/foul stack.

fourth_quarter.read: The collapse point. Knicks trailed 93-71 with 7:52 left, then Brunson attacked Harden relentlessly and the Knicks produced a 30-8 closing regulation stretch to tie 101-101.
fourth_quarter.model_note: This was the miss. The LiveFlow engine failed to trigger COMEBACK_TSUNAMI_MODE when Knicks cut the lead from 22 to single digits with Brunson hunting Harden and Cleveland offense stagnating.

overtime.read: Knicks opened OT with a 9-0 run and finished +11 in the extra period. Cleveland had no stable creation: 1/7 FG, 1/6 from three, three turnovers, zero rebounds credited in OT team table before team rebounds.
overtime.model_note: OT margin expansion destroyed both anti-blowout tickets. Future NO margin bets require overtime blowout-tail protection in games with late collapses.

Jalen Brunson.diagnostic: Primary comeback engine. Brunson hunting Harden in the fourth was the decisive matchup shift.

Donovan Mitchell.diagnostic: Elite through three quarters but late-game shot creation faded under Knicks pressure and fatigue/foul context.

James Harden.diagnostic: Assist prop failure and defensive target. Harden usage created drag: more turnovers than assists and more turnovers than made field goals.

Josh Hart.diagnostic: Rebound ladder failed due to minutes compression and late rotation shape. Hart played only 2:01 in Q4 and 0:42 in OT.

Karl-Anthony Towns.diagnostic: Rebound funnel and late possession hub, but turnover volatility remains high.

OG Anunoby.diagnostic: Late OT value spike. Despite poor early offense, he became the free-throw/defensive stabilizer in OT.

Landry Shamet.diagnostic: Hidden rotation swing. Bench shooting changed late spacing and reduced Hart closing minutes.

new_patch_queue.trigger: Any NO-favorite-margin ticket live in a tied or overtime-risk playoff game must include OT margin expansion tail. If comeback team has momentum and crowd edge, do not assume close regulation equals close final margin.
Activate when a home playoff team cuts a 18+ point fourth-quarter deficit to single digits with 4:30+ remaining and primary closer is generating paint touches or foul pressure.
If Harden is the target defender in repeated late-game actions and has 4+ turnovers or 4+ fouls, downgrade Cleveland closeout probability and widen opponent late-run distribution.
Do not ladder Hart rebounds unless projected closing lineup has Hart above 34 minutes or Knicks are not using Shamet/spacing-heavy closing units.
When Cleveland has 15+ turnovers entering late fourth and relies on Harden/Mitchell isolation, downgrade win probability and team total conversion even if leading by double digits.

NYK: Shamet spacing can replace Hart in closing stretches
CLE: Allen offensive rebound presence active but free throw volatility remains
